title: Noun といわず Noun といわず
titleEn: Expresses 'both A and B' or 'be it A or B'; applies to both without distinction.
structure: Noun1 + といわず + Noun2 + といわず + (rest of the sentence)
structureEn: Noun1 + といわず + Noun2 + といわず + (rest of the sentence)
explanation: Expresses 'both A and B' or 'be it A or B'; applies to both without distinction. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar pattern AといわずBといわず means 'be it A or B' or 'both A and B.' It emphasizes that something applies widely, without distinguishing between A and B.

title: Noun + ときたら
titleEn: Expresses strong feelings about a topic; 'speaking of', 'when it comes to'.
explanation: Expresses strong feelings about a topic; 'speaking of', 'when it comes to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun + ときたら emphasizes strong feelings—often frustration, admiration, or surprise—about that noun. It can translate as 'when it comes to...', 'speaking of...', or 'honestly, about...'.

title: Noun とは比べものにならない
titleEn: Indicates something is not comparable; 'beyond comparison'.
structure: Noun + とは比べものにならない
structureEn: Noun + とは比べものにならない
explanation: Indicates something is not comparable; 'beyond comparison'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Use とは比べものにならない to emphasize that two things differ so greatly they cannot be compared—one is vastly superior or different in scale, quality, etc.

title: Noun + ともあろう + Noun
titleEn: Used to criticize or question unbecoming behavior by someone of a certain status.
explanation: Used to criticize or question unbecoming behavior by someone of a certain status. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun + ともあろう + Noun is used to express that someone with a certain role or status has done something unbefitting their position. It often carries disapproval or surprise: 'For someone who is supposed to be X, how could they...?'

title: Noun ともなると
titleEn: 'When it comes to...', emphasizes a change of situation or status.
explanation: 'When it comes to...', emphasizes a change of situation or status. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun + ともなると indicates a change of state or circumstances once that noun becomes relevant or once one attains that position. It conveys that 'once it’s X, things change' or 'when it comes to X...'.

title: Noun ともなれば
explanation: Expresses 'if it comes to', 'when', or 'once'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ともなれば is used to stress the likelihood of a change or consequence that comes about due to the situation expressed before ともなれば. It can be translated as 'when it comes to', 'once', or 'if it comes to' in English. It's usually used with nouns.

title: Noun と相まって
titleEn: Used to express 'in combination with', 'together with', 'along with'.
explanation: Used to express 'in combination with', 'together with', 'along with'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Noun と相まって grammar point is used to indicate that something happens or is the result of two or more factors working together. It can be translated as 'in combination with', 'together with' or 'along with' in English. It is used with nouns.

title: Noun なくして～はない
titleEn: Expresses 'without ~, there can't be ~'; 'can't do ~ without first doing ~'.
structure: Noun + なくして + Sentence ～はない
structureEn: Noun + なくして + Sentence ～はない
explanation: Expresses 'without ~, there can't be ~'; 'can't do ~ without first doing ~'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The なくして grammar point is used to indicate that something is indispensable or necessary for something else to occur. 'Without' the first noun, the second part 'cannot exist/occur'. It demonstrates the principle of causality or necessity.

title: Noun なしでは～ない
titleEn: Expresses the necessity of something; 'cannot do without', 'not possible without'.
explanation: Expresses the necessity of something; 'cannot do without', 'not possible without'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun なしでは～ない is used to emphasize that something is indispensable. It can be translated as 'cannot do without' or 'not possible without.'

title: Noun なしには～ない
explanation: Means 'without', 'cannot do without', 'not possible without'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The なしには～ない grammar point is used to express that without the mentioned noun, something is not possible. It can be translated as 'cannot (do) without ~', or 'not possible without ~'.
